title: Secondary adoption, retirement, and afterlife sources

files.title: Microsoft Health Common User Interface - CodePlex Archive landing page
files.trust_note: Recovered from the public CodePlex archive. Useful for release provenance, but it is a preservation site rather than the original CodePlex host.

files.title: Microsoft Health Common User Interface - CodePlex Archive releases page
files.trust_note: Recovered from the public CodePlex archive. Captures release-version history and attached package names.

files.title: Microsoft Health Common User Interface - CodePlex Archive license page
files.trust_note: Recovered from the public CodePlex archive and useful for confirming the published Ms-PL license.

files.title: Microsoft Health Common User Interface - CodePlex Archive source-code tree page
files.trust_note: Recovered from the public CodePlex archive. Useful mainly for understanding the limits of the surviving archive because the exposed tree is very thin and direct attachment URLs currently reject unauthenticated fetches.

files.title: EMIS becomes first GP supplier to commit to CUI
files.trust_note: Industry press source, not a primary design source, but useful evidence of early supplier adoption claims.

files.title: Microsoft recognises EMIS and CareWorks
files.trust_note: Industry press source useful for evidence of recognition and adoption claims, including the iLink HealthVault/CUI implementation mention.

files.title: Telerik forum reference to pjd.mscui.net PrimaryCare demonstrator
files.trust_note: Third-party forum discussion. Useful only as narrow corroboration that `pjd.mscui.net/PrimaryCare.htm` was publicly reachable in 2010.

files.title: GovLoop tweet archive referencing the Patient Journey Demonstrator
files.trust_note: Third-party tweet archive. Useful only as a light corroborating reference that the Patient Journey Demonstrator URL was being shared publicly in 2010.

files.title: Microsoft launches CUI internationally
files.trust_note: Industry press source useful because it adds a conservative contemporary reading of NHS adoption depth at launch, including the point that EMIS was the largest supplier then implementing CUI elements.

files.title: Aintree sticks with System C
files.trust_note: Industry press source useful as a concrete trust deployment example tying Medway Sigma usage to significant CUI element adoption.

files.title: System C interim/final results statement on Medway Sigma and CUI
files.trust_note: Investor-relations source from the supplier itself. It is not neutral, but it is stronger than second-hand commentary for claims about the product line and its stated CUI standardization.

files.title: Indra lleva a la Comunidad Valenciana a la vanguardia de la salud digital
files.trust_note: Browser-rendered capture of a current Indra-hosted article because plain HTTP fetches were challenged. Useful for the Valencia/ABUCASIS lead, but extraction may include translation and runtime chrome.

files.title: Microsoft health patient journey demonstrator
files.trust_note: PubMed abstract page for a peer-reviewed article describing an exemplar implementation and reporting it increased interest in the programme.

files.title: HCI-modelling for improving the clinical usability of digital health technologies
files.trust_note: PubMed abstract page for a recent peer-reviewed article explicitly combining NHS CUI and the NHS Design System.

files.title: Civica Care Records product page
files.trust_note: Vendor product page. Not neutral evidence, but useful as a current explicit reference to CUI guidance surviving in product marketing and positioning.

files.title: EPaCCS Recommended IT System Requirements v1.5
files.trust_note: Later NHS requirements document showing that CUI standards and guidance were still being invoked in 2015.

files.title: Palliative care co-ordination: core content. Requirements Specification
files.trust_note: Later NHS requirements specification showing CUI references persisted into downstream programme requirements.
